title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 13 - Forest of the Dead Expansion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1M5hO5Iz4hQ

Daniel (@dungeondive) examines the Forest of the Dead, the first big-box expansion for Forbidden Fortress and home to what he considers the best tile art in the entire Shadows of Brimstone line. This shadowy realm between worlds is a haunted forest where the dead from all dimensions roam among decayed trees and cold fog.

Four enemy types arrive: goryu (undead myth-beasts with defense 4 whose critical hits only reduce defense to 2), raiona vampires (serpent women in five broods — Blood Vipers, Reptilia Conclave, Medusan Reavers, Sisters of the Night, Black Rattle), spectral hordes (whose combat scales with the target's spirit stat and defense equals the attacker's cunning), and shadow wraiths (three variants — lantern, sword, gun — each with wraith relics and haunted memories, potentially becoming legendary spectres like Shinmo Tanaka the Headless Samurai).

The "Lost Souls" and "Roaming Dead" threat cards let players fight any enemy in their collection as undead or ghost versions, dramatically expanding replayability. Daniel highlights two standout missions: "Spectres of the Past" and "The Broken Amulet" (a four-piece puzzle hunt), calling them among the best in the entire game. An optional rule lets dead campaign heroes return as legendary spectre mini-bosses. The Haunted Castle world card turns standard Forbidden Fortress missions into ghost-infested nightmares.

----

Would you use the optional rule to fight your fallen campaign heroes as legendary spectres?
